name: legacycrypt

metadata.name: legacycrypt
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Python Software Foundation License
metadata.summary: Wrapper to the POSIX crypt library call and associated functionality.

documentation:
# Legacy crypt — Function to check Unix passwords

The legacycrypt module is a standalone version of
https://docs.python.org/3/library/crypt.html

This module implements an interface to the crypt(3) routine, which is a
one-way hash function based upon a modified DES algorithm; see the Unix man
page for further details. Possible uses include storing hashed passwords so
you can check passwords without storing the actual password, or attempting
to crack Unix passwords with a dictionary.

Notice that the behavior of this module depends on the actual
implementation of the crypt(3) routine in the running system. Therefore,
any extensions available on the current implementation will also be
available on this module.

modules.name: legacycrypt
documentation: Wrapper to the POSIX crypt library call and associated functionality.

functions.name: legacycrypt._crypt_crypt
documentation:
Hash a *word* with the given *salt* and return the hashed password.

[clinic input]
    crypt.crypt

    word: str
    salt: str
    /

*word* will usually be a user's password.  *salt* (either a random 2 or 16
character string, possibly prefixed with $digit$ to indicate the method)
will be used to perturb the encryption algorithm and produce distinct
results for a given *word*.

returns Py_BuildValue("s", crypt_result)

functions.name: legacycrypt.mksalt
documentation:
Generate a salt for the specified method.

If not specified, the strongest available method will be used.

functions.name: legacycrypt.crypt
documentation:
Return a string representing the one-way hash of a password, with a salt
prepended.

If `salt` is not specified or is `None`, the strongest
available method will be selected and a salt generated. Otherwise,
`salt` may be one of the `crypt.METHOD_*` values, or a string as
returned by `crypt.mksalt()`.

classes.name: legacycrypt._crypt_data_libcrypt
legacycrypt._crypt_data_libxcrypt
legacycrypt._Method
documentation:
Class representing a salt method per the Modular Crypt Format or the
legacy 2-character crypt method.
